name: Cross-Site Scripting: Comment Reflection
description: A feedback form's preview page reflects user comments back as HTML, with template auto-escape disabled. The flag is exposed as a JS global on the same page.

hint_tiers.text: Look at how the comment is passed into the template. Is anything telling Jinja 'this string is already safe HTML, don't escape it'?
Reflected XSS — the comment is rendered without escaping. Try a comment containing a <script> tag and see if it survives in the rendered HTML.

Try <script>document.body.innerText = window.__INTERNAL_NOTES</script>. The fix is to remove the Markup wrapper so Jinja's auto-escape applies — or use a sanitizer like bleach.clean with an allow-list.
